steps.title: Spot the pervasiveness
steps.content: The passage labels this bias a “dominant view” needing a “necessary corrective”—clearly signaling that the bias is widespread throughout the field.

steps.title: Match the meaning to the answer
steps.content:
The blank needs to mean spreads throughout — the pro-innovation bias is present across studies of technology history, requiring a “necessary corrective.” Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — supersedes: “a bias that supersedes studies” — supersedes means replaces or takes the place of. The bias doesn’t replace studies; it runs through them. ✗
  • Choice B — pervades: “a bias that pervades studies” — pervades means spreads throughout and is present everywhere in something. The passage calls this the “dominant view,” confirming it permeates the field. ✓
  • Choice C — abridges: “a bias that abridges studies” — abridges means shortens or condenses. A bias doesn’t shorten studies. ✗
  • Choice D — foments: “a bias that foments studies” — foments means stirs up or incites trouble. The bias doesn’t incite studies into existence; it runs through existing ones. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
